title: pavonia gallaensis
intro: Pavonia gallaensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the waxflower or rock rose. Native to tropical Africa, this plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pavonia gallaensis include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
